$comment: puzzleDrag2 Balance Profile — the machine-readable per-game state the /game-balance skill reads on load. Theory lives in the skill's handbook; this file holds ONLY game-specifics. Keep it in sync after a balance pass (skill workflow step 9).
genre: single-player, turn-based, transitive economy (tile-drag match/merge farming with a zone-tier progression spine)
generatedFrom: codebase exploration + the campaign pacing harness

goals.$status: captured 2026-06-22 via the skill's goals interview
goals.targetPlayer: mid-core (multiple sessions/day) as the baseline segment; casual & power on either side
goals.sessionShape: ~2-3 sessions/day; a session ~= one farm run (~10 turns)
goals.intendedTimeToContent: STEADY ~1-2 weeks to mid-game (found 2nd settlement + grow home to City/tier 3) -> ~20-35 mid-core sessions
goals.feel: balanced middle — cozy surface, light strategy; enough economic tension that choices matter, but few hard walls
constraints: save-compatibility — value tweaks are safe; structural/persisted-shape changes need a SAVE_SCHEMA bump + migration
don't silently break the family-value or campaign snapshot tests (update them intentionally)

kpiTargets.$note: Derived from goals by working backward (mid-core ~2.5 sessions/day, steady ~1-2 weeks). Deliberately rough; refine against the campaign sim once it models buildings/crafting/mine. 'current' = what the harness/trace measures today.

home_found_2_affordable_coins.current: ~2 (coins arrive too fast to be a real gate; the true gate is completing home)

home_tier1_hamlet.current: REACHABLE (softlock fixed 2026-06-23; bread bakes from farm goods at Outpost). Run-count not yet validated by the pacing sim.

home_tier2_village.current: REACHABLE (re-costed to farm goods). Run-count not yet validated.

home_tier3_city_MIDGAME.current: REACHABLE (full ladder opens on farm goods). Run-count not yet validated.

knobs.name: TILES_PER_RESOURCE
knobs.controls: income divisor: tiles chained per 1 inventory unit

knobs.name: UPGRADE_THRESHOLDS
knobs.controls: board upgrade gate: chains to upgrade a tile (decoupled from income)

knobs.name: BUILDINGS[].cost
knobs.controls: building construction cost (gates completion + tier climb)

knobs.name: ZONES[].entryCost
knobs.controls: coins to start a session in a zone (home=50)

knobs.name: tiers[].upgradeCost
knobs.controls: settlement tier-up cost (coins + zone-inventory resources; rung 0 free)

knobs.name: requiresZoneTier
knobs.controls: zone unlock gate (e.g. quarry requires home tier 3) — the lever for the progression-stall finding

knobs.name: boards.farm.seasonDrops / upgradeMap
knobs.controls: per-zone tile spawn rates + chain-upgrade redirects

formulas.chainCoins: perChain = max(1, floor(chainLength * tileValue)) + coinHookBonus; then * coin_gain_mult boon. Source: src/state.ts:454. ~LINEAR in chain length.
formulas.resourceAccrual: progress[res] += chainLength; whole units = floor(progress / TILES_PER_RESOURCE[tile]); remainder carried. Source: src/state.ts:392-416.
formulas.realizedValuePerTile: ITEMS[res].value / TILES_PER_RESOURCE[tile]  — the dominant-strategy / distance-from-power-curve metric (family-value spread audit).
formulas.foundingCost: round(300 * 1.7^(k-1)) for the k-th founding -> 300, 510, 867, 1474, ... EXPONENTIAL sink. Source: src/features/zones/data.ts:404.
formulas.netCoinsPerRun: chainCoins(earned this run) - entryCost(50 home) + SEASON_END_BONUS_COINS(25) + buildingBonuses.
formulas.turnBudget: max(1, floor((baseTurns + additive + bonus) * (useFertilizer?2:1))). Home baseTurns=10. Source: src/features/zones/data.ts:192.

converters: board upgradeMap (chain a tile -> spawn a higher-tier tile)

harness.perRun: npm run playtest -- --zones home --runs 10 --seed 1   (coins/run, coins/turn, family-value spread, change-list)
harness.campaign: npm run playtest -- --campaign --zones home --runs 30 --seed 1   (runs-to-coin-milestone + tier-stall pacing, by playing)
harness.progression: npm run playtest -- --progression   (CODE-DERIVED spine: fresh-save reachability + per-zone siloed oracle + softlock detection, no runs played. Rewrites the progression-timeline SPINE+DIFF blocks + emits progression.json/progression-diff.json)
harness.progressionDiff: every --progression run diffs the fresh spine vs the committed baseline (reference/docs/balance/progression.baseline.json — the last REVIEWED spine, tracked) and flags what moved since (softlock created/cleared + reachability flips = critical; gate/wall = major; cost tweak = minor); the dashboard marks changed facts OUTDATED. `--progression --accept` promotes current → baseline (the snapshot-test model: review the diff, then accept).
harness.progressionMap: reference/docs/balance/progression-timeline.html   (the interactive alignment artifact — handbook §10; Overview banner + Changes tab show the cross-run diff)
harness.tests: npx vitest run src/__tests__/playtest-harness.test.ts   (determinism + per-run/campaign/progression-shape snapshot drift guards)
harness.code: src/playtest/  (run.ts per-run loop, campaign.ts pacing loop, progression.ts structural oracle, metrics.ts spread, targets.ts bands)
harness.knownGap: the campaign PACING sim is FARM-board-only and doesn't model crafting/buildings/mine, so it can't yet validate a stall FIX end-to-end by playing. The progression.ts oracle now covers the STRUCTURE (reachability + softlock) statically and pins it in CI; extending the pacing sim to cross zones is the remaining tooling task.

zoneCoinTargets.$note: deliberately wide v1 bands; tighten from a real pass

progressionTargets.$note: updated toward the steady ~1-2 week goal; was [2,12]/[4,20]

findings.resolution: FIXED 2026-06-23. Town 1 made self-contained on farm goods: Bakery re-costed block:6 -> hay_bundle:6 and its unlock moved Hamlet -> Outpost [constants.ts BUILDINGS; cartography/data.ts home tiers]; home Village & City upgradeCost re-costed off ALL mine goods to farm goods only (bread/pie/soup/eggs/plank/flour/hay_bundle). Quarry gate left at home=City (Zone 2 = farm+mine opens AFTER Town 1 is grown). Verified: progression oracle flips RED->green (home maxSelfTier 0/3 -> 3/3, every board zone reachable), baseline re-accepted (progression.baseline.json), 21/21 playtest-harness tests pass (progression-shape snapshot updated intentionally), lint/typecheck/build green. New downstream (non-blocking) wall: quarry Smeltworks->Foundry City needs gold_bar.
findings.summary: A fresh save is HARD-SOFTLOCKED at home Outpost (tier 0). The first rung, Hamlet, needs `bread`; bread is crafted only at the Bakery; the Bakery (a) costs `block` to build and (b) only UNLOCKS at Hamlet — the rung it gates. Three stacked locks, all rooted in mine goods being demanded before the mine is reachable: (A) Bakery build cost includes block:6 [constants.ts:788]; (B) Bakery unlocks at home tier 1, whose gate is bread:10 [cartography/data.ts:264-265] — circular; (C) home Village/City rungs need block/coke/iron_bar [cartography/data.ts:273,282]. Mine goods come only from mine boards, and EVERY mine/fish zone is gated behind the quarry (quarry requiresZoneTier {home, 3}; harbor/caves/forge require the quarry) [cartography/data.ts:344,392,419,446]. Almost certainly collateral from PR #1299 (zones-as-spine) not reconciling the authored home tier/building costs.
findings.confirmed: HARD LOCK — now CODE-DERIVED, not just traced. The progression oracle (src/playtest/progression.ts; `npm run playtest -- --progression`) computes fresh-save reachability = {home, meadow, orchard, crossroads} (all farm/no-board) and detects the wall at home Outpost->Hamlet on `bread`. CARTO/TRAVEL also enforces the travel gate (slice.ts:78). The escape-valve audit found NO out: orders/daily-rewards/story grants/init give no mine goods or bread; inventory is siloed per zone so another zone's output can't pay home's tier-up; no tier-0 home building yields bread or mine goods.
findings.marketLatentEscape: The ONE near-miss: MARKET_PRICES lists block/iron_bar/coke/bread as buyable and BUY_RESOURCE doesn't itself check the caravan post [market.ts:72-80], but the buy UI only shows once caravan_post is built [Inventory.tsx:299,510] — and caravan_post costs block+iron_bar. So the market is a latent escape that is itself mine-gated. Ungating the buy UI (or seeding an early buyable starter good) is the cheapest single lever.
findings.lever: Pick one+ (analysis only — not applied): (1) re-cost the Bakery off `block` so bread is craftable at Outpost from farm goods; (2) move the Bakery's unlock to Outpost (tier 0); (3) lower the quarry's requiresZoneTier from {home,3} [cartography/data.ts:344] so the mine opens early; (4) re-cost home Village/City off mine goods; (5) ungate the market buy UI / seed a starter import. The progression-shape snapshot test will flag whichever fix re-opens the spine.
findings.source: code-derived progression oracle (src/playtest/progression.ts) + building-cost/gate trace + escape-valve audit; visualised in reference/docs/balance/progression-timeline.html

findings.summary: Realized value-per-tile spans ~10x (pearls ~133/tile vs pie ~12.9/tile; anything >3x median flagged). The per-run harness already emits a compression change-list toward a 3-4x ceiling.

Coins reach the founding price in ~2 runs — far faster than the steady ~1-2 week goal. Coins are NOT the pacing gate today (progression resources are). For the 'balanced middle' feel, consider making coins a softer secondary gate (raise founding/tier coin costs or trim an early faucet) once progression is unblocked.

openQuestions: RESOLVED 2026-06-23: the home progression softlock is fixed (Town 1 self-contained on farm goods) — see findings[0].resolution.

NEXT SESSION is a live design conversation (see reference/docs/balance/index.html Overview > 'Discussion agenda'). Scope: keep a ROUGH whole-game outline, but deeply design+balance only the FIRST TWO ZONES; balance target = 'feels balanced through the FOUNDING OF THE THIRD ZONE'; zone 3+ open.

Zone-2 board model: can ONE settlement own both a farm and a mine board, and how does the player switch? (home is farm-only, quarry mine-only today — feasibility unverified). Zone 2 = farm+mine teaches the rations->mine-turns combination; its mine is HAZARD-FREE (hazards are later zones).

Workers (CRITICAL): wire houses -> workers -> shorter on-board chain length (effectiveMinChain floors at 3, reads only the boss; workers currently reduce the income threshold, not chain length). Per-zone pool vs the existing global villagers pool? Magnitudes?

Building-ability specials layer is ALREADY BUILT (14+ effect types via the aggregator) — the work is ASSIGNING specials to buildings per zone as balance levers, plus any new channel (chain-length; an active 'Extra Turn').

Zone completion / capstone: reframe the 'sub-boss' as a big capstone PROJECT (e.g., launch a Fair; the fairground node is a stub)? Permanent GLOBAL abilities don't exist yet (new machinery) — build now, or keep the first two zones on the existing keeper->Hearth-Token path?
Token gate: keep 'one of each biome (farm/mine/harbor) -> Old Capital', or move to N-of-M?
Zone roster & twists: the 8 board zones exist but are undifferentiated — keep all or consolidate, and which 2-3 unique twists to build first vs. high-level placeholders?

Value spread (~10x -> 3-4x ceiling via the harness change-list) and coin pacing (coins too fast; make them a soft secondary gate via the action-start/entry-cost sink) — deep tune deferred to the cross-zone pacing sim.
Confirm constraints: no real-money; saves must survive value tweaks.
